mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month payroll growth above 100k signals robust labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k, or the signal's IC drops below 0.10 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, creating positive momentum in equity markets that persists for 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce positive returns in 50%+ of cases, or individual series show negative correlations with SPX in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing activity, which correlates with improved corporate margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO, or the signal's IC falls below 0.05 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation below 2% (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank complacency, leading to accommodative monetary policy that supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL remains below 2.0, or the signal's IC drops below 0.08 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDP (GDPC1) during a high-unemployment regime (UNRATE > 8%) signals economic distress, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails in 50%+ of cases, or individual series show no significant correlation with SPX during high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 8% during a manufacturing expansion (INDPRO > 95) creates a positive feedback loop between labor market tightness and corporate earnings, driving 5-day SPX gains.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails in 50%+ of cases, or individual series show negative correlations with SPX in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 95 during low-interest-rate environments (FEDFUNDS < 1%) indicates strong real economic activity, generating positive 20-day SPX returns.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails in 50%+ of cases, or the combined effect disappears when FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.0% in 3-year rolling windows.
